info.name: Lantern
info.summary: Browse server-side git repositories inside Nextcloud
info.description:
Lantern is a read-only browser for git repositories, behind your existing Nextcloud login. Browse the file tree, read files with syntax highlighting, render READMEs, view history with diffs and blame, switch branches/tags, search in files, and preview images.
Four kinds of repository, one uniform UI: - Server-side repositories an administrator configures (with optional per-group access restrictions). - Git repositories inside a user's own Nextcloud Files. - GitHub repositories (read-only, via a personal access token for private repos). - GitLab repositories, including self-hosted instances (read-only, via a personal access token for private projects).
Also integrates with Nextcloud unified search and the dashboard. Read-only by design — Lantern never modifies a repository.
Requires the git binary to be installed and runnable by the web-server user for server-side and Files repositories. The official Nextcloud Docker image does not include git; Lantern's setup check will warn you if it is missing.
info.author: Paul Ammann
